figures.caption: Figure 5: Results of the experiment in Section 5.5 using the adaptation of integrators (20b) and (20c) to coupled systems. The DOF per direction are (nρ, nθ, nϕ) = (30, 50, 30) (total number of DOF 2 · 45000 + 2 · 1500), and the number of time steps is m = 200000. Top: u component (left) and isosurface at level 1.5 (right) at the final time. Bottom: r component at final time (left) and evolution of integral means ⟨u⟩ and ⟨r⟩ (right). The total simulation wall-clock time is about 6.5 minutes.

Figure 7: Results for the r component of the experiment in Section 5.6 using the adaptation of integrators (20a) and (20d) to coupled systems. The DOF per direction are (nρ, nθ, nz) = (160, 160, 20) (total number of DOF 2 ·512000+ 2 · 25600), and the number of time steps is m = 8000. Left: r component at the final time. Right: evolution of integral mean ⟨r⟩. The total simulation wall-clock time is about 4 minutes.

Figure 3: Results of the experiment in Section 5.3 using a suitable adaptation of integrator (23). The DOF per direction are (nρ, nθ) = (160, 160) (total number of DOF 2 · 25600), and the number of time steps is m = 25000. Left: u component at the final time. Right: evolution of integral mean ⟨u⟩. The total simulation wall-clock time is about 19 seconds.

Figure 2: Results of the experiment in Section 5.2 using integrator (23). The DOF per direction are (nρ, nθ) = (40, 80) (total number of DOF 2 · 3200), and the number of time steps is m = 10000. Left: u component at the final time. Right: evolution of integral mean ⟨u⟩. The total simulation wall-clock time is about 1 second.
Figure 8: Results for the u component of the experiment in Section 5.6 using the adaptation of integrators (20a) and (20d) to coupled systems, see also Figure 7.

Figure 4: Results of the experiment in Section 5.4 using the adaptation of integrator (20b) to coupled systems. The DOF per direction are (nθ, nϕ) = (100, 50) (total number of DOF 2 · 5000), and the number of time steps is m = 9000. Left: u component at the final time. Right: evolution of integral mean ⟨u⟩. The total simulation wall-clock time is about 3 seconds.

Figure 1: Results of the experiment in Section 5.1 for varying number of time steps m. The DOF in the top plots are (nρ, nθ) = (25, 50), while in the bottom plots are (nρ, nθ) = (40, 80). The reference dashed line has slope −1. In the legend, FE denotes the forward Euler method, EE the exponential Euler scheme (24), and SplitEE the proposed split exponential Euler integrator (23).
